report_name: Ransomware Landscape Report: Qilin Leads, 'The Gentlemen' Surges in Q1 2026

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: The most active ransomware group in Q1 2026, claiming 361 victims. The group is noted for its speed of execution from initial compromise to encryption. Its activity has decreased by 25% compared to Q4 2025.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A relatively new ransomware group, first appearing in August 2025, which saw a dramatic surge in activity in Q1 2026 to become the second most active operator with 182 victims.

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A previously prominent ransomware group whose activity declined by 22% in Q1 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups encrypt critical data on victim networks to force a ransom payment.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Modern ransomware strains, such as those used by Qilin, delete volume shadow copies or other local backups to hinder recovery efforts, often using vssadmin commands.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: As part of a standard double extortion tactic, ransomware groups exfiltrate sensitive files to a leak site before encrypting data, threatening to publish the data if the ransom is not paid.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups continue to rely on phishing as a common initial access vector.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups gain initial access by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing services such as VPNs and RDP.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: A ransomware payload deployed by the Qilin group, known for its rapid encryption capabilities. It is associated with the creation of files with the '.qilin' extension.

malware.name: Akira
malware.description: Ransomware associated with the Akira threat group, which saw a decline in activity in Q1 2026.

identities.name: Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: The manufacturing sector was the most heavily impacted industry by ransomware attacks in Q1 2026.

identities.name: Construction Industry
identities.description: The construction industry saw a notable 44% year-over-year increase in ransomware attacks in Q1 2026.

infrastructure.name: Ransomware Leak Site
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used by ransomware groups for double extortion tactics, where exfiltrated sensitive data is hosted with the threat of public release.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e3f-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/ransomware-reaches-elevated-new-normal-as-attack-volumes-hold-steady-into-2026-reshape-baseline-risk-expectations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare for deleting all shadow copies.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the command used to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Qilin Ransomware File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: Qilin Ransomware File Extension Detected
id: f9e8d7c6-b5a4-4987-6543-210fedcba987
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the '.qilin' extension, indicating Qilin ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/ransomware-reaches-elevated-new-normal-as-attack-volumes-hold-steady-into-2026-reshape-baseline-risk-expectations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.qilin'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - None expected.
level: critical
indicators.description: Detects files being created with the .qilin extension, a strong indicator of an active Qilin ransomware infection.
